rationale: Demonstrating irreversible commitment deters external intervention and strengthens bargaining leverage despite economic costs.
Targeted strikes on command nodes can degrade resistance without triggering full escalation, preserving options for controlled pressure.
Projecting force offshore threatens blockade capabilities and secures sea lines for potential energy rerouting or coercion.
Disabling critical infrastructure control systems creates asymmetric pressure without kinetic escalation, buying time for political resolution.
Covert disruption of energy transit nodes undermines Tethys’ revenue stability and weakens their ability to resist economically.
Fabricating internal subversion threats can fracture Tethys’ domestic unity and justify our continued presence as "stabilization."
Securing reserves of critical energy inputs now cushions against pipeline disruptions and insulates the domestic economy from supply shocks.
Devaluing the national currency improves export competitiveness to offset lost energy revenues and counters sanction effects.
Mobilizing domestic capital through patriotic financing instruments sustains military spending without triggering inflationary spiral or austerity backlash.
Exploit Novaris’s logistical strain with a precision strike on forward supply depots to disrupt their frontline momentum.
Position naval forces near Tethys pipeline terminals to signal our capability to interdict energy flows if necessary.
Conduct large-scale cyber-physical readiness drills to mask true force posture while boosting domestic morale.
Use deep-cell operatives to disable key rail nodes in Novaris’s rear echelon, exacerbating their logistical overextension.
Disrupt Novaris military logistics networks by infiltrating and degrading their transport scheduling systems.
Stage a minor, deniable provocation to discredit Novaris’s narrative of victimhood and fracture international support.
Rally liberal democracies by framing Novaris’s aggression as a systemic threat to energy transit norms.
Propose neutral-party mediation through the Global Accord to position us as peace-seeking while buying strategic time.
Open monitored aid routes to highlight civilian suffering under Novaris pressure and sway global opinion.
Halt all critical mineral exports to Novaris to exploit their supply chain fragility in defense manufacturing.
Secure reserves of fuel and food to extend our endurance beyond projected breaking point of the war economy.
Flood offshore markets with Novaris-linked financial instruments to amplify inflationary pressures and erode confidence.
